name: 7 UI principles (checklist)
description: Hierarchy, progressive disclosure, consistency, contrast, proximity, accessibility, alignment — a fast UI checklist for reviews.

metadata.intent: Apply a practical UI principles checklist to improve clarity and reduce cognitive load

conventions: Review flows step-by-step; ensure each step has one primary action and clear escape hatch.

steps.description: Hierarchy: can users identify the primary action and the next step in <5 seconds? Use size/weight/spacing/contrast to guide attention.
Progressive disclosure: reveal complexity as needed; orient users with step indicators so they know where they are and what's left.
Consistency: same components look/behave the same across screens. Deviations must have a strong reason (e.g., critical destructive action).
Contrast: highlight critical information/CTAs; also meet accessibility contrast needs (foreground/background).
Proximity: things that belong together are grouped together; dangerous actions separated from safe controls.
Accessibility: keyboard navigation, focus states, alt text, labels, sufficient target sizes, and readable contrast. Prefer `pi validate` checks before new rules.
Alignment: grids and consistent edges reduce cognitive load; misalignment should be intentional and rare.
